message:
Implement GraphQL batched repository discovery (bd-ff8h)

Efficiently discovers open issues and PRs across all configured repos
using GraphQL alias batching (up to 25 repos per API call).

Added functions:
- chunk_repo_ids() - Split repos into batches of 25
- gh_api_graphql_repo_batch() - Build and execute aliased GraphQL query
- parse_graphql_work_items() - Parse JSON response to TSV using jq
- repo_spec_to_github_id() - Convert repo spec to owner/repo format
- discover_work_items() - Full implementation replacing stub

Features:
- Filters archived and forked repos automatically
- Extracts issues, PRs, labels for scoring
- Supports max_repos limit
- Graceful degradation if jq/gh missing

Tested: `ru review --dry-run` successfully discovers work items.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

-{"id":"bd-ff8h","title":"Implement GraphQL batched repository discovery","description":"# Task: Implement GraphQL Batched Repository Discovery

## Purpose
Efficiently discover open issues and PRs across all configured repos using GraphQL alias batching, reducing API calls from O(n) to O(n/25).

## Background: Why GraphQL Batching?
- REST API: 1 call per repo for issues + 1 for PRs = 2n calls
- GraphQL aliases: Query 25 repos in single call = n/25 calls
- For 100 repos: 200 REST calls → 4 GraphQL calls (50× reduction)
- Avoids rate limiting, faster discovery

## Implementation Details

### gh_api_graphql_repo_batch()
```bash
gh_api_graphql_repo_batch() {
    local chunk="$1"
    local q="query {"
    local i=0

    while IFS= read -r repo_id; do
        [[ -z "$repo_id" ]] && continue
        local owner="${repo_id%%/*}"
        local name="${repo_id#*/}"

        q+=" repo${i}: repository(owner:\"${owner}\", name:\"${name}\") {"
        q+=" nameWithOwner isArchived isFork updatedAt"
        # Issues with metadata for scoring
        q+=" issues(states:OPEN, first:50, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt"
        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
        q+="   }"
        q+=" }"
        # PRs with metadata for scoring
        q+=" pullRequests(states:OPEN, first:20, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt isDraft"
        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
        q+="   }"
        q+=" }"
        # Oldest open issue for staleness
        q+=" oldestIssue: issues(states:OPEN, first:1, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:ASC}) {"
        q+="   nodes { createdAt }"
        q+=" }"
        q+=" }"
        ((i++))
    done <<< "$chunk"

    q+=" }"
    
    gh api graphql -f query="$q" 2>/dev/null
}
```

### parse_graphql_work_items()
Uses jq to flatten response into TSV for easy Bash parsing:
```bash
parse_graphql_work_items() {
    local resp="$1"

    echo "$resp" | jq -r '
        .data | to_entries[] | select(.value != null) |
        select(.value.isArchived != true) |
        select(.value.isFork != true) |
        .value as $repo |
        (
            # Issues
            ($repo.issues.nodes // [])[] |
            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "issue", .number, .title,
             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
             .createdAt, .updatedAt, "false"] | @tsv
        ),
        (
            # PRs
            ($repo.pullRequests.nodes // [])[] |
            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "pr", .number, .title,
             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
             .createdAt, .updatedAt, (.isDraft | tostring)] | @tsv
        )
    ' 2>/dev/null
}
```

### chunk_repo_ids()
Split repo list into chunks of 25:
```bash
chunk_repo_ids() {
    local chunk_size="$1"
    shift
    local repos=("$@")
    local count=0
    local chunk=""
    
    for repo in "${repos[@]}"; do
        chunk+="$repo"$'\n'
        ((count++))
        if [[ $count -ge $chunk_size ]]; then
            echo "$chunk"
            chunk=""
            count=0
        fi
    done
    
    [[ -n "$chunk" ]] && echo "$chunk"
}
```

### discover_work_items()
Main discovery function that:
1. Gets all repos from config
2. Resolves to owner/repo format
3. Filters non-GitHub hosts
4. Chunks into batches of 25
5. Executes GraphQL queries
6. Parses responses into work items
7. Calculates priority scores
8. Sorts by score descending
9. Applies max_repos limit if specified

## Data Format
Work item string format (pipe-separated):
```
repo_id|type|number|title|score|level|created_at|updated_at
owner/repo|issue|42|Authentication failing|85|HIGH|2024-12-15T...|2025-01-01T...
```

## Error Handling
- Skip repos that fail resolution
- Continue on individual query failures
- Log warnings for rate limit responses
- Graceful degradation if GraphQL unavailable

## Dependencies
- jq for JSON parsing (already assumed by ru)
- gh CLI with GraphQL support

## Testing
- Mock GraphQL response with fixture
- Verify chunking works correctly
- Verify archived/forked repos filtered
- Verify TSV parsing handles edge cases
- Verify 25-repo chunks respected

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Single API call for up to 25 repos
- [ ] Issues and PRs both discovered
- [ ] Labels extracted for scoring
- [ ] Archived/forked repos excluded
- [ ] Handles 100+ repos efficiently","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.462046988-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:45:11.008373378-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-ff8h","depends_on_id":"bd-mnu9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:44:52.851431049-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

Uses jq to flatten response into TSV for easy Bash parsing:

    local resp="$1"

    echo "$resp" | jq -r '

        (
            # PRs
            ($repo.pullRequests.nodes // [])[] |
            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "pr", .number, .title,
             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),

        )
    ' 2>/dev/null
}
```

Split repo list into chunks of 25:

chunk_repo_ids() {
    local chunk_size="$1"
    shift

    local chunk=""
    
    for repo in "${repos[@]}"; do
        chunk+="$repo"$'\n'

        if [[ $count -ge $chunk_size ]]; then
            echo "$chunk"
            chunk=""
            count=0
        fi
    done
    
    [[ -n "$chunk" ]] && echo "$chunk"
}
```

Main discovery function that:
1. Gets all repos from config
2. Resolves to owner/repo format
3. Filters non-GitHub hosts
4. Chunks into batches of 25
5. Executes GraphQL queries
6. Parses responses into work items
7. Calculates priority scores
8. Sorts by score descending
9. Applies max_repos limit if specified

## Data Format
Work item string format (pipe-separated):
```
repo_id|type|number|title|score|level|created_at|updated_at
owner/repo|issue|42|Authentication failing|85|HIGH|2024-12-15T...|2025-01-01T...
```

## Error Handling
- Skip repos that fail resolution
- Continue on individual query failures
- Log warnings for rate limit responses
- Graceful degradation if GraphQL unavailable

## Dependencies
- jq for JSON parsing (already assumed by ru)
- gh CLI with GraphQL support

## Testing

- Verify 25-repo chunks respected

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Issues and PRs both discovered
- [ ] Labels extracted for scoring
- [ ] Archived/forked repos excluded
- [ ] Handles 100+ repos efficiently","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:16:53.462046988-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T16:48:51.213603357-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T16:48:51.213603357-05:00","close_reason":"GraphQL batched discovery implemented: chunk_repo_ids(), gh_api_graphql_repo_batch(), parse_graphql_work_items(), repo_spec_to_github_id(), discover_work_items().  {"id":"bd-fi65","title":"Implement basic mode TUI (gum-based fallback)","description":"Task: Implement Basic Mode TUI (gum-based)

Purpose
-------
Provide a simple but functional TUI when advanced dashboard not available,
using gum for styled output and selection.

When Used
---------
- gum available but not full TUI mode
- User prefers simpler interface
- Debugging/development

Implementation
--------------

show_question_basic_mode()
  Display single question with gum styling:
  
  gum style --border rounded --padding "1 2" \
    --border-foreground "#fab387" \
    "Question from: $repo"
  
  echo "$question"
  
  Build options array from JSON
  gum choose "${option_labels[@]}"

show_discovery_summary_basic()
  gum style --border rounded "Discovery Summary"
  echo "Repos with activity: $count"
  echo "Total work items: $items"
  echo "High priority: $high"

show_session_status_basic()
  Simple text status:
  for session in sessions:
    echo "$repo: $state"

basic_mode_loop()
  Sequential question processing:
  while questions pending:
    show_question_basic_mode
    answer = gum choose
    send_answer
    mark_answered

gum_confirm_wrapper()
  with fallback to read prompt:
  
  if GUM_AVAILABLE:
    gum confirm "$prompt"
  else:
    read -rp "$prompt [Y/n] " yn
    case "${yn,,}" in y|yes) return 0 ;; esac

Progress Display
----------------
gum spin --title "Processing..." -- command
gum style --foreground "green" "✓ Done"

Formatting Helpers
------------------
format_priority_badge()
  case priority:
    CRITICAL: gum style --foreground "red" "CRITICAL"
    HIGH: gum style --foreground "orange" "HIGH"
    NORMAL: gum style --foreground "yellow" "NORMAL"
    LOW: gum style --foreground "gray" "LOW"

ANSI Fallback
-------------
If gum not available, use ANSI codes:
- Bold: \033[1m
- Colors: \033[31m (red), etc.
- Box drawing: Unicode ─│┌┐└┘

Testing
-------
- Verify gum styling renders
- Verify choice selection works
- Verify ANSI fallback works
- Test without gum installed

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] Questions display with styling
- [ ] Options selectable via gum choose
- [ ] Progress shown with gum spin
- [ ] Works without gum (ANSI fallback)
- [ ] Keyboard shortcuts work","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:40.686170236-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:40:40.686170236-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-fi65","depends_on_id":"bd-4ps0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:13.195307516-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

All 28 tests pass."}
@@ -181,7 +181,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-jen3","title":"Implement review command exit codes and error classification","description":"# Task: Implement Review Command Exit Codes

## Purpose
Define and implement consistent exit codes for `ru review` following the ru exit code conventions, enabling automation and scripting.

## Background: ru Exit Code Convention
From AGENTS.md, ru uses these exit codes:
- 0 = Success (all repos synced/reviewed)
- 1 = Partial failure (some repos failed)
- 2 = Conflicts exist (needs resolution)
- 3 = Dependency/system error (gh missing, auth failed)
- 4 = Invalid arguments
- 5 = Interrupted (use --resume)

## Review-Specific Exit Codes

### Exit 0: Success
- All sessions completed successfully
- All questions answered
- All approved changes applied (if --apply)

### Exit 1: Partial Failure
- Some sessions failed but others succeeded
- Some repos could not be reviewed (network, rate limit)
- Partial progress was made

### Exit 2: Conflicts/Review Needed
- Agent detected merge conflicts in worktree
- Quality gates failed (tests/lint)
- Changes require human intervention before apply

### Exit 3: Dependency/System Error
- Claude Code not installed or not in PATH
- gh CLI not authenticated
- tmux not available (for local driver)
- ntm not available (when --mode=ntm specified)

### Exit 4: Invalid Arguments
- Unknown flags
- Invalid --mode value
- Invalid --priority value
- Conflicting options (--plan with --push)

### Exit 5: Interrupted
- SIGINT/SIGTERM received
- --max-runtime exceeded
- --max-questions exceeded
- Checkpoint saved, use --resume to continue

## Implementation

### Error Classification Function
```bash
classify_review_error() {
    local error_type="$1"
    local context="$2"
    
    case "$error_type" in
        session_failed|rate_limited|network_error)
            echo "partial"  # Exit 1
            ;;
        merge_conflict|quality_gate_failed|tests_failed)
            echo "conflict"  # Exit 2
            ;;
        missing_dependency|auth_failed|no_driver)
            echo "system"  # Exit 3
            ;;
        invalid_flag|bad_mode|conflicting_options)
            echo "invalid"  # Exit 4
            ;;
        interrupted|timeout|max_questions)
            echo "interrupted"  # Exit 5
            ;;
        *)
            echo "unknown"
            ;;
    esac
}
```

### Exit Code Aggregation
```bash
aggregate_exit_code() {
    local -a exit_codes=("$@")
    local max_code=0
    
    for code in "${exit_codes[@]}"; do
        [[ $code -gt $max_code ]] && max_code=$code
    done
    
    # Special case: 5 (interrupted) takes precedence over 1 (partial)
    if [[ " ${exit_codes[*]} " =~ " 5 " ]]; then
        echo 5
        return
    fi
    
    echo $max_code
}
```

### Final Exit Handling
```bash
finalize_review_exit() {
    local exit_code="$1"
    
    case "$exit_code" in
        0) log_success "Review completed successfully" ;;
        1) log_warn "Review completed with partial failures" ;;
        2) log_error "Review blocked by conflicts - manual resolution needed" ;;
        3) log_error "Review failed due to system/dependency error" ;;
        4) log_error "Invalid arguments" ;;
        5) log_warn "Review interrupted - use --resume to continue" ;;
    esac
    
    exit "$exit_code"
}
```

## Testing
- Verify each exit code for its conditions
- Verify --resume works after exit 5
- Verify aggregation chooses worst code
- Verify CI can parse exit codes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 exit codes defined and documented
- [ ] Error classification works correctly
- [ ] Exit code aggregation from multiple sessions
- [ ] Clear log message for each exit code
- [ ] --resume only available after exit 5

 {"id":"bd-jm89","title":"Define unified session driver interface","description":"# Task: Define Unified Session Driver Interface

## Purpose
Create an abstract interface that both ntm and local drivers implement, enabling graceful degradation and consistent behavior regardless of which driver is used.

## Background: Why a Unified Interface?
- ntm provides advanced orchestration but may not be installed
- Local driver (tmux + stream-json) works everywhere
- Same code should work with either driver
- Enables testing without full ntm dependency
- Future drivers (remote, cloud) can plug in

## Interface Definition

### Driver Capabilities
```bash
# Query what driver can do
driver_capabilities() {
    # Returns JSON with capabilities
    cat << EOF
{
  "name": "local",
  "parallel_sessions": true,
  "activity_detection": false,
  "health_monitoring": false,
  "question_routing": true
}
EOF
}
```

### Core Operations
```bash
# Start a new Claude session
# Args: worktree_path, session_name, initial_prompt
# Returns: session_id
driver_start_session() {
    local wt_path="$1"
    local session_name="$2"
    local prompt="$3"
    # Implementation varies by driver
}

# Send message/answer to session
# Args: session_id, message
# Returns: 0 on success
driver_send_to_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local message="$2"
}

# Get current session state
# Args: session_id
# Returns: JSON with state info
driver_get_session_state() {
    local session_id="$1"
    # Returns: {"state": "generating|waiting|complete|error", ...}
}

# Stop/kill a session
# Args: session_id
driver_stop_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}

# Interrupt session (Ctrl+C equivalent)
# Args: session_id
driver_interrupt_session() {
    local session_id="$1"
}
```

### Event Streaming
```bash
# Stream events from session
# Args: session_id, callback_function
# Callback receives: event_type, event_data
driver_stream_events() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local callback="$2"
    # Calls: $callback "question" "$question_json"
    # Calls: $callback "complete" "$status"
    # Calls: $callback "error" "$error_msg"
}
```

### Normalized Event Schema
Both drivers emit the same event format:
```json
{
  "type": "init|generating|waiting|complete|error",
  "session_id": "...",
  "timestamp": "2025-01-04T10:30:00Z",
  "wait_info": {
    "reason": "ask_user_question|agent_question_text|external_prompt|unknown",
    "context": "...",
    "options": ["a) ...", "b) ..."],
    "recommended": "a",
    "risk_level": "low|medium|high"
  }
}
```

## Driver Selection
```bash
detect_review_driver() {
    # Try ntm first (preferred)
    if command -v ntm &>/dev/null; then
        if ntm --robot-status &>/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_verbose "Using ntm driver (robot mode available)"
            echo "ntm"
            return
        fi
        log_verbose "ntm found but robot mode unavailable"
    fi

    # Fall back to local driver
    if command -v tmux &>/dev/null && command -v claude &>/dev/null; then
        log_verbose "Using local driver (tmux + stream-json)"
        echo "local"
        return
    fi

    log_error "No suitable driver available"
    echo "unsupported"
}
```

## Driver Loading
```bash
load_review_driver() {
    local driver="$1"
    
    case "$driver" in
        ntm)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/ntm.sh"
            ;;
        local)
            source "$RU_LIB_DIR/drivers/local.sh"
            ;;
        *)
            log_error "Unknown driver: $driver"
            return 1
            ;;
    esac
    
    REVIEW_DRIVER="$driver"
}
```

## Implementation Notes
- Functions prefixed with driver_ are implemented per-driver

-{"id":"bd-k1kx","title":"Implement ntm driver using robot mode API","description":"Task: Implement ntm Driver Using Robot Mode API

Purpose
-------
Implement the unified session driver interface using ntm robot mode,
providing advanced orchestration capabilities beyond the local driver.

ntm Robot Mode API
------------------
ntm exposes a JSON API via command-line flags:

1. --robot-spawn SESSION_NAME
   Create new tmux session with Claude panes
   Returns: {"success": true, "session": "...", "panes": [...]}

2. --robot-status
   Query all session states
   Returns: {"sessions": {...}, "questions": [...], "system": {...}}

3. --robot-health=SESSION
   Get health metrics for session
   Returns: {"agents": {...}, "alerts": [...]}

4. --robot-activity=PANE_ID
   Get activity state for specific pane
   Returns: {"state": "GENERATING", "velocity": 12.5, "confidence": 0.85}

5. --robot-route=SESSION
   Get recommended pane for new work
   Returns: {"recommendation": {"pane_id": "...", "reason": "..."}}

6. --robot-send --pane=PANE_ID --msg=MESSAGE
   Send message to pane
   Returns: {"status": "ok", "delivered": true}

Implementation
--------------

ntm_driver_start_session()
  - Call ntm --robot-spawn with --cc=$parallel for Claude panes
  - Record pane assignments in state
  - Return session info JSON

ntm_driver_get_session_state()
  - Call ntm --robot-activity for pane
  - Map ntm states to unified states
  - Include wait_info if WAITING

ntm_driver_send_to_session()
  - Call ntm --robot-send with pane and message
  - Verify delivery confirmation

ntm_driver_stream_events()
  - Poll --robot-activity periodically
  - Detect state transitions
  - Call callback on significant events

ntm_driver_stop_session()
  - Use tmux kill-session (ntm manages cleanup)

State Mapping
-------------
ntm state      -> Unified state
GENERATING     -> generating
WAITING        -> waiting (with wait_info)
THINKING       -> thinking
STALLED        -> stalled
ERROR          -> error

Health Monitoring Integration
-----------------------------
Start background health monitor:
  while session active:
    health = ntm --robot-health
    for alert in health.alerts:
      handle_alert(alert)
    sleep 10

Advantages Over Local Driver
----------------------------
- Velocity-based detection (more accurate)
- Health monitoring built-in
- Routing for optimal pane selection
- Better stall detection
- Structured error patterns

Testing
-------
- Mock ntm commands for unit tests
- Integration test with real ntm
- Verify state mapping correct
- Verify health alerts handled

Acceptance Criteria
-------------------
- [ ] All driver interface methods implemented
- [ ] State mapping matches unified schema
- [ ] Health monitoring integrated
- [ ] Routing recommendation used
- [ ] Graceful fallback if ntm unavailable","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:45.086444687-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T15:38:45.086444687-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-k1kx","depends_on_id":"bd-jm89","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T15:45:11.685975198-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# STREAM-JSON EVENT PARSING (bd-8zt6)
+# Parse Claude Code's --output-format stream-json NDJSON output
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+# Parse a single stream-json event line
+# Args:
+#   $1 - JSON line to parse
+#   $2 - nameref for event_type output
+#   $3 - nameref for event_data output
+# Returns:
+#   0 if valid JSON, 1 if invalid
+parse_stream_json_event() {
+    local line="$1"
+    local -n _pse_event_type=$2
+    local -n _pse_event_data=$3
+
+    # Validate JSON
+    if ! echo "$line" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
+        _pse_event_type="invalid"
+        _pse_event_data="$line"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    _pse_event_type=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.type // "unknown"')
+
+    case "$_pse_event_type" in
+        system)
+            local subtype
+            subtype=$(echo "$line" | jq -r '.subtype // ""')
+            if [[ "$subtype" == "init" ]]; then
+                _pse_event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '{session_id, tools, cwd}')
+            else
+                _pse_event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.')
+            fi
+            ;;
+        assistant)
+            _pse_event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.message.content // []')
+            ;;
+        user)
+            _pse_event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '.message.content // []')
+            ;;
+        result)
+            _pse_event_data=$(echo "$line" | jq -c '{status, duration_ms, session_id, cost_usd}')
+            ;;
+        *)
+            _pse_event_data="$line"
+            ;;
+    esac
+
+    return 0
+}
+
+# Detect if an assistant event contains AskUserQuestion tool use
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Event data (message.content array)
+# Returns:
+#   0 if AskUserQuestion found, 1 otherwise
+detect_ask_user_question() {
+    local event_data="$1"
+
+    # Check if any content block is AskUserQuestion
+    echo "$event_data" | jq -e \
+        '.[] | select(.type == "tool_use" and .name == "AskUserQuestion")' \
+        >/dev/null 2>&1
+}
+
+# Extract question information from AskUserQuestion tool use
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Event data (message.content array)
+# Outputs:
+#   JSON object with question details
+extract_question_info() {
+    local event_data="$1"
+
+    # Extract the AskUserQuestion input
+    local question_input
+    question_input=$(echo "$event_data" | jq -c \
+        '[.[] | select(.name == "AskUserQuestion")] | .[0].input // {}')
+
+    # Parse first question (usually only one)
+    local tool_use_id
+    tool_use_id=$(echo "$event_data" | jq -r \
+        '[.[] | select(.name == "AskUserQuestion")] | .[0].id // ""')
+
+    # Format for queue
+    echo "$question_input" | jq --arg tool_id "$tool_use_id" '{
+        questions: .questions,
+        tool_use_id: $tool_id,
+        detected_at: (now | todate)
+    }'
+}
+
+# Detect questions in plain text output (fallback detection)
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Text to check for question patterns
+# Returns:
+#   0 if question pattern found, 1 otherwise
+detect_text_question() {
+    local text="$1"
+
+    # Question patterns
+    local -a patterns=(
+        'Should I'
+        'Do you want'
+        'Would you like'
+        'Please confirm'
+        'Choose.*:'
+        'Which.*\?'
+        'What.*\?'
+        'How should'
+        '\[y/N\]'
+        '\[Y/n\]'
+        'Enter.*:'
+        'Press.*to'
+    )
+
+    for pattern in "${patterns[@]}"; do
+        if echo "$text" | grep -qiE "$pattern"; then
+            return 0
+        fi
+    done
+
+    return 1
+}
+
+# Extract text content from assistant message
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Event data (message.content array)
+# Outputs:
+#   Plain text extracted from text blocks
+extract_text_content() {
+    local event_data="$1"
+
+    echo "$event_data" | jq -r \
+        '[.[] | select(.type == "text") | .text] | join("\n")'
+}
+
+# Check if event contains tool use
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Event data (message.content array)
+# Outputs:
+#   Tool names used (newline-separated)
+get_tool_uses() {
+    local event_data="$1"
+
+    echo "$event_data" | jq -r \
+        '[.[] | select(.type == "tool_use") | .name] | .[]'
+}
+
+# Process stream-json events from a file/pipe
+# Args:
+#   $1 - Path to log file or named pipe
+#   $2 - Callback function name (receives: event_type, event_data)
+# Returns:
+#   0 on successful completion, 1 on error
+process_stream_json() {
+    local source="$1"
+    local callback="$2"
+
+    # Validate callback is a function
+    if ! declare -F "$callback" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
+        log_error "process_stream_json: callback '$callback' is not a function"
+        return 1
+    fi
+
+    while IFS= read -r line; do
+        [[ -z "$line" ]] && continue
+
+        local event_type event_data
+        if ! parse_stream_json_event "$line" event_type event_data; then
+            log_verbose "Skipping invalid JSON line"
+            continue
+        fi
+
+        case "$event_type" in
+            system)
+                "$callback" "init" "$event_data"
+                ;;
+            assistant)
+                # Check for AskUserQuestion
+                if detect_ask_user_question "$event_data"; then
+                    local question_info
+                    question_info=$(extract_question_info "$event_data")
+                    "$callback" "question" "$question_info"
+                else
+                    # Check for text content with question patterns
+                    local text_content
+                    text_content=$(extract_text_content "$event_data")
+                    if [[ -n "$text_content" ]] && detect_text_question "$text_content"; then
+                        "$callback" "text_question" "$text_content"
+                    else
+                        "$callback" "assistant" "$event_data"
+                    fi
+                fi
+                ;;
+            user)
+                "$callback" "user" "$event_data"
+                ;;
+            result)
+                "$callback" "complete" "$event_data"
+                break
+                ;;
+            *)
+                "$callback" "unknown" "$event_data"
+                ;;
+        esac
+    done < "$source"
+}
+
 # Local driver: Stream events from a session
 # Parses NDJSON from Claude's stream-json output
 local_driver_stream_events() {

+    # Prefer pipe if exists, otherwise use log file with tail -f
+    if [[ -p "$event_pipe" ]]; then
+        process_stream_json "$event_pipe" "$callback"
+    elif [[ -f "$log_file" ]]; then
+        # Use tail -f for real-time streaming
+        tail -f "$log_file" | process_stream_json /dev/stdin "$callback"
+    else
+        log_error "No event source found for session: $session_id"
+        return 1
+    fi

+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# GRAPHQL BATCHED REPOSITORY DISCOVERY (bd-ff8h)
+#
+# Efficiently discovers open issues and PRs across all configured repos
+# using GraphQL alias batching (up to 25 repos per API call).
+#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
+
+# Split repo IDs into chunks for batched GraphQL queries
+# Args: chunk_size, repo_ids...
+# Output: Each chunk on a separate line (repos within chunk are newline-separated)
+chunk_repo_ids() {
+    local chunk_size="$1"
+    shift
+    local repos=("$@")
+    local count=0
+    local chunk=""
+
+    for repo in "${repos[@]}"; do
+        chunk+="${repo}"$'\n'
+        ((count++))
+        if [[ $count -ge $chunk_size ]]; then
+            printf '%s' "$chunk"
+            chunk=""
+            count=0
+        fi
+    done
+
+    # Output remaining repos
+    [[ -n "$chunk" ]] && printf '%s' "$chunk"
+}
+
+# Execute GraphQL query for a batch of repos
+# Args: chunk (newline-separated repo IDs like owner/repo)
+# Output: GraphQL JSON response
+gh_api_graphql_repo_batch() {
+    local chunk="$1"
+    local q="query {"
+    local i=0
+
+    while IFS= read -r repo_id; do
+        [[ -z "$repo_id" ]] && continue
+        local owner="${repo_id%%/*}"
+        local name="${repo_id#*/}"
+
+        # Build aliased query for this repo
+        q+=" repo${i}: repository(owner:\"${owner}\", name:\"${name}\") {"
+        q+=" nameWithOwner isArchived isFork updatedAt"
+        # Issues with metadata for scoring
+        q+=" issues(states:OPEN, first:50, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
+        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt"
+        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
+        q+="   }"
+        q+=" }"
+        # PRs with metadata for scoring
+        q+=" pullRequests(states:OPEN, first:20, orderBy:{field:CREATED_AT, direction:DESC}) {"
+        q+="   nodes { number title createdAt updatedAt isDraft"
+        q+="     labels(first:10) { nodes { name } }"
+        q+="   }"
+        q+=" }"
+        q+=" }"
+        ((i++))
+    done <<< "$chunk"
+
+    q+=" }"
+
+    # Execute query via gh CLI
+    gh api graphql -f query="$q" 2>/dev/null
+}
+
+# Parse GraphQL response into work items (TSV format)
+# Args: json_response
+# Output: TSV lines: repo_id\ttype\tnumber\ttitle\tlabels\tcreated_at\tupdated_at\tis_draft
+parse_graphql_work_items() {
+    local resp="$1"
+
+    echo "$resp" | jq -r '
+        .data | to_entries[] | select(.value != null) |
+        select(.value.isArchived != true) |
+        select(.value.isFork != true) |
+        .value as $repo |
+        (
+            # Issues
+            ($repo.issues.nodes // [])[] |
+            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "issue", .number, .title,
+             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
+             .createdAt, .updatedAt, "false"] | @tsv
+        ),
+        (
+            # PRs
+            ($repo.pullRequests.nodes // [])[] |
+            [$repo.nameWithOwner, "pr", .number, .title,
+             ([.labels.nodes[].name] | join(",")),
+             .createdAt, .updatedAt, (.isDraft | tostring)] | @tsv
+        )
+    ' 2>/dev/null
+}
+
+# Convert repo spec to owner/repo format (GitHub only)
+# Args: repo_spec
+# Output: owner/repo or empty if not GitHub
+repo_spec_to_github_id() {
+    local spec="$1"
+    local url branch custom_name local_path repo_id
+
+    # Parse spec to get repo_id
+    if resolve_repo_spec "$spec" "$PROJECTS_DIR" "$LAYOUT" url branch custom_name local_path repo_id; then
+        # Check if it's a GitHub repo
+        if [[ "$url" =~ github\.com[/:] ]]; then
+            echo "$repo_id"
+        fi
+    fi
+}
+

+    # Convert to GitHub IDs (filter non-GitHub repos)
+    local -a github_repos=()
+    for spec in "${all_repos[@]}"; do
+        local github_id
+        github_id=$(repo_spec_to_github_id "$spec")
+        if [[ -n "$github_id" ]]; then
+            github_repos+=("$github_id")
+        fi
+    done
+
+    if [[ ${#github_repos[@]} -eq 0 ]]; then
+        log_verbose "No GitHub repos configured"
+        return 0
+    fi
+
+    log_verbose "Querying ${#github_repos[@]} GitHub repo(s)"
+
+    # Process in chunks of 25
+    local chunk_size=25
+    local all_work_items=""
+    local chunk=""
+    local count=0
+
+    for repo in "${github_repos[@]}"; do
+        chunk+="${repo}"$'\n'
+        ((count++))
+
+        if [[ $count -ge $chunk_size ]]; then
+            # Execute batch query
+            log_verbose "Querying batch of $count repos"
+            local response
+            if response=$(gh_api_graphql_repo_batch "$chunk"); then
+                local items
+                items=$(parse_graphql_work_items "$response")
+                [[ -n "$items" ]] && all_work_items+="${items}"$'\n'
+            else
+                log_warn "GraphQL batch query failed"
+            fi
+            chunk=""
+            count=0
+        fi
+    done
+
+    # Process remaining repos
+    if [[ -n "$chunk" ]]; then
+        log_verbose "Querying final batch of $count repos"
+        local response
+        if response=$(gh_api_graphql_repo_batch "$chunk"); then
+            local items
+            items=$(parse_graphql_work_items "$response")
+            [[ -n "$items" ]] && all_work_items+="${items}"$'\n'
+        else
+            log_warn "GraphQL batch query failed"
+        fi
+    fi
+
+    # Parse work items into array (pipe-separated format)
+    # Format: repo_id|type|number|title|labels|created_at|updated_at|is_draft
+    while IFS=$'\t' read -r repo_id item_type number title labels created_at updated_at is_draft; do
+        [[ -z "$repo_id" ]] && continue
+        # Convert TSV to pipe-separated for easier parsing later
+        _items_ref+=("${repo_id}|${item_type}|${number}|${title}|${labels}|${created_at}|${updated_at}|${is_draft}")
+    done <<< "$all_work_items"
+
+    # Apply max_repos limit if specified
+    if [[ -n "$max_repos" ]] && [[ ${#_items_ref[@]} -gt $max_repos ]]; then
+        _items_ref=("${_items_ref[@]:0:$max_repos}")
+    fi
+
+    log_verbose "Discovered ${#_items_ref[@]} work item(s)"
 }
 
 # Stub: Show discovery summary (will be enhanced in later phases)
